mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and declining corporate profit margins, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60d returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are negative in >60% of rising INDPRO months, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index indicate a tightening monetary policy response to inflation, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20d returns in >58% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are negative in >62% of periods with positive payroll growth, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth crosses below 1.0 percent, it indicates a severe demand contraction that forces corporate earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of low-growth quarters, or the threshold effect is not robust to alternative GDP revisions.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in industrial production and decline in the unemployment rate confirms a synchronized labor and output expansion, which drives short-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5d returns in >57% of cases, or the predictive power disappears when tested on high-volatility market regimes.
